title: Widen skeleton: reindex, STATUS, context, and Markdown views
description: Close the generated-artifacts layer. Add `waystation reindex` to rebuild the bun:sqlite index from canonical JSON + events, and generate the human/agent-facing Markdown: STATUS.md, the compact context/*.md files, and the one-way JSON->Markdown record views under views/ (spec §16). All of these are rebuildable, non-canonical outputs.
acceptance: `waystation reindex` rebuilds .waystation/index.sqlite from canonical files and events; deleting and rebuilding yields equivalent query behavior.
`waystation report` regenerates .waystation/reports/STATUS.md from the ledger (fully replacing the hand-written STATUS.md).
`waystation context` regenerates .waystation/context/active-work.md and blocked.md from the ledger.
Generated files carry a 'do not edit — generated by waystation' header so humans/agents know not to hand-edit them.
summary.md is NOT overwritten by generation (it holds hand-authored architecture/spike notes); if generated at all it must use a separate file or a clearly delimited managed block.
One-way JSON->Markdown record views under views/**/*.md are generated; generation is never reversed.
bun:test covers an index rebuild producing stable query results and STATUS/active-work generation matching the ledger.
notes: AUTOMATION NOTE (requested 2026-07-06): STATUS.md and active-work.md must become generated artifacts, not hand-maintained — this task owns that. Depends on the mutation path (real state) and the brief (context assembly). Guard: summary.md currently contains valuable hand-written content (architecture boundary, Graphify notes, stack spike results) — do NOT clobber it; treat it as hand-maintained or introduce a separate generated file. Confirm the timestamp format (see issue-timestamp-format) before generating time-stamped reports so output is consistent.
